South_Korea.status: DRAW OR WIN = safe advance to 2nd. Even loss = may advance as 3rd.

South_Africa.status: MUST WIN + need CZE to not win OR win by larger margin

qualification_math.South_Korea: A DRAW advances them to 2nd place (guaranteed). Win = 1st in group (ahead on GD). They do NOT need to win.
qualification_math.South_Africa: Must win. AND need CZE to drop points, OR win by bigger margin than CZE.

rsa_gd1_vs_mex.note: RSA stats heavily distorted by going to 9 men at 49'

rsa_gd2_vs_cze.note: RSA DOMINATED possession (68%) and shots (17), scored late. Better than Elo suggests.

kor_gd1_vs_cze.son_stats: 69 minutes, 6 shots, 1 SOT (SOT = YES, but only 1 in 69 min)

kor_gd2_vs_mex.note: Korea dominated but lost. GK error cost them. Son substituted - possible precaution.

Son_Heung_min.expected_gd3: STARTING, expected full 90 — chasing WC goals (56 int'l, 2 from all-time KOR record)
Son_Heung_min.rule15_note: KOR is the DOMINANT team (58.8% win) — no forward suppression applies

Oswin_Appollis.expected_gd3: STARTING — primary attacking outlet for RSA
Oswin_Appollis.rule15_note: RSA at 15.7% win (borderline RULE7) — mild suppression applied

Teboho_Mokoena.status: SUSPENDED — 2nd yellow in GD2. Critical loss for RSA midfield (their DM/engine).

Themba_Zwane.status: SUSPENDED — red card in GD1. Another creative/attack player missing.

Lee_Kang_in.note: On 1 yellow — caution risk with Tello refereeing

Kim_Min_jae.note: KOR's CB anchor — excellent defensive reading

referee.name: Facundo Tello
referee.card_rate_note: HIGH CARD RATE — infamous for refereeing 10-red-card Boca vs Racing Champions Trophy Final
referee.impact: Elevate card-related questions. Q2 (KOR card 2H) gets Tello uplift.

rules_applied.RULE8: FIRES (barely) — draw 25.50% ≥ 25%. Mild shrink of extreme attacking props. Less aggressive than CZE-MEX since draw is barely over threshold.
rules_applied.RULE7: Does NOT fire — RSA 15.69% > 15%. Borderline. Monitor.
rules_applied.RULE15: Does NOT fully fire for Appollis (RSA 15.69% > 15%). Mild near-RULE15 applied.
rules_applied.RULE15_SON: DOES NOT APPLY — Son plays for the EXPECTED WINNER (KOR 58.8%). Dominant team forward = no suppression.
rules_applied.RULE_FOULS: RSA is underdog, defends → fouls more. MODERATED: Mokoena (main DM/fouler) SUSPENDED.
rules_applied.TELLO_EFFECT: High-card referee. Elevates P(cards) for Q2. Tello is likely to show cards in tense physical RSA defending vs KOR attackers.
rules_applied.RSA_SUSPENSIONS: Mokoena + Zwane both OUT. Weakens RSA midfield and attack. Reduces: BTTS probability, RSA fouls (Mokoena was their main fouler), RSA 3+ SOT slightly.

Q1.question: Will South Korea be caught offside 2 or more times?
Q1.rationale: Model 0.871, RULE8 mild. KOR was highly active in GD1 (15 shots, 6 SOT). RSA midfield depleted (Mokoena/Zwane suspended) → KOR runs freely. KOR plays high line = offsides come from quick runs.

Q2.question: Will South Korea receive at least 1 card in the second half?
Q2.rationale: Facundo Tello = high-card referee. Lee Kang-in on 1 yellow (caution risk). RSA's physical desperate defending will draw KOR fouls. But KOR only needs a draw = might not take risks. Near coin-flip.
Q2.warning: Very small crowd edge. Tello uplift justifies YES_LEAN.

Q3.question: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
Q3.rationale: Model 0.313, RULE8. RSA missing Mokoena/Zwane weakens their attack. KOR defense (Kim Min-jae) is excellent. RSA had 4 SOT in GD2 but against much weaker CZE. BEST EDGE in RSA-KOR.

Q4.question: Will South Korea have more shots on target than South Africa in the second half?
Q4.rationale: Model 0.844. KOR much stronger, Son playing full 90. RSA without key midfielders — less creative threat. KOR will dominate 2H even if score is level (they hold to secure draw = less desperate pressing). Strong YES.

Q5.question: Will South Africa commit more fouls than South Korea?
Q5.rationale: RULE_FOULS: RSA is underdog (15.7%), defends → fouls more. MODERATED from 0.65 because Mokoena (their main DM/fouler) is suspended. RSA GD2 had 68% possession — they can attack too.

Q6.question: At halftime, will the match be tied?
Q6.rationale: Model HT-tie P=0.403 (P(0-0)=0.302, P(1-1)=0.093). KOR more likely to score early with RSA's depleted midfield. Slightly below model. RULE8 mild pushes toward draw family.

Q7.question: Will South Africa have 3 or more shots on target?
Q7.rationale: Model 0.390. RSA had 4 SOT in GD2 vs CZE (they CAN do 3+, even exceeded it). Must-win = aggressive attack. But Mokoena/Zwane absent weakens creative output. Near-model estimate.

Q8.question: Will Oswin Appollis have at least 1 shot on target?
Q8.rationale: Model 0.390. Primary RSA wide attacker, must-win = gets forward. BUT: RSA at 15.7% win (near RULE15), KOR defense is excellent. Club form good (11G+9A). No confirmed WC SOT data.

Q9.question: Will Son Heung-min have at least 1 shot on target?
Q9.rationale: Model 0.846. GD1 vs CZE: 6 shots, 1 SOT in 69 min (YES outcome). Expected full 90 today vs much weaker RSA (missing Mokoena/Zwane). RULE15 does NOT apply (KOR is the dominant team). Son chasing WC record goals.

post_match_results.platform_note: Platform renumbered Q6-Q10 from our JSON order. Our Q10 (RSA win) was platform Q6; our Q6-Q9 shifted to platform Q7-Q10. Results mapped back to our original question numbering.
key_learnings: RSA 1-0 KOR — MASSIVE UPSET. Model had RSA at 15.7% win. Mokoena+Zwane both suspended. RSA still won. Once-in-a-tournament underdog result. Our 0.17 estimate was the correct approach (closest credible YES answer) but still a large loss (-5.47).

Son SOT (Q9) = NO — Son in a DOMINANT team context (58.8% win probability) still went 0 SOT. This extends the Son-SOT-0 pattern to even non-RULE15 contexts. RSA's desperate defending (even without Mokoena) shut down Son entirely. NEW OBSERVATION: Son's 6-shots/1-SOT conversion rate in GD1 suggested shot-quality issues. Consider lowering Son SOT estimates systematically.

KOR 2+ offsides (Q1) = NO — our 0.70 was very wrong (-19.17 RBP). KOR was the dominant team but apparently didn't get caught offside 2+ times. In retrospect, RSA's low block defending (not a high line) reduces offside trap opportunities. Dominant team running into a deep defensive block ≠ high offside count.

RSA 3+ SOT (Q7) = YES — despite suspensions, RSA had 3+ shots on target in a match they WON. They needed to attack to win; the desperation + must-win context drove their shot volume. Our 0.40 was too low vs the must-win context.

RSA more fouls (Q5) = NO — Mokoena's absence plus RSA's winning possession may have meant KOR fouled more while chasing. RULE_FOULS fired but was wrong. When RSA ended up CONTROLLING the match (given they won), the foul-more-underdog prior inverted.
BTTS NO confirmed (Q3) — KOR scored 0 goals. Our 0.30 estimate was correct direction. RSA defensive solidity (despite depleted midfield) denied KOR. Best result of the match (+5.66).
HT tied = YES (Q6) — the 1-0 final score suggests RSA scored in 2H (or at end of 1H). Match was 0-0 at HT, consistent with a late RSA winner.
